title: Sexualization of Minor characters in Evangelion
display_title: Sexualization of Minor characters in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Sexualization of Minor characters in Evangelion             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 31, 2004 9:37 pm Warning: This thread may contain some mature imagery and discussion of a sexual nature. Please be advised. This discussion is stemming from the following topic: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=285 Anyone who has been an Eva fan for a time knows about the sexualization of the minor characters in the show. This is the case for quite a few anime. Basically, if its a female character who is old enough to have gone through puberty, its fair game. Its odd, at times disgusting, and at times horribly innapropriate. Some of this is official, some is fans taking it to another level to satiate their childish fantasies. Now, as I said, part of this is Gainax's fault, as they put just as much sexualization on the 14 year old characters as the adult characters. No better example can be given than the masturbation scene in End of Evangelion. http://www.evamonkey.com/just_plain_wrong/ This is taken one step further with official spin-off games with some stuff that could be interpretted as fantasizing on the part of those involved, or catering to those with those fantasies. http://www.evamonkey.com/game/ And lets not forget that Gainax has made (or licensed) some soft core eva hentai. I'm not certain if the images in this next directory are Gainax done, but I'm pretty sure. I do know that there is worse than this that is Gainax done/approved. http://www.evamonkey.com/soft_core/ And finally, own your very own minor in sexually suggestive presentation. Included in this directory are Bedtime Asuka and Rei (spawned from the bedsheet designs), as well as the life size Rei and Asuka "action figures". http://www.evamonkey.com/evil_merche/ I personnally concede that is impossible to avoid adding the slightest sexualization to a minor character. We are sexual creatures at some fundamental level, and as such, there is some sexualization lent at some level, even if it is unintended. But it is possible to limit this. And it is not neccessary to cater to the desires of fans. I have never condoned the secondary representation of the characters, nor do I understand to its full extent. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Aug 31, 2004 9:49 pm cool, i didnt know Hikari was into plad panties

[]: Re: Sexualization of Minor characters in Evangelion             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:03 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Basically, if its a female character who is old enough to have gone through puberty, its fair game.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:05 pm my stomach turned after trying to find Asuka pics for someone go google, "Asuka" images. and look at the first one ><!

[]:             Quote Post by Treeves056 » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:31 pm Its not that bad considering the other stuff Then again like Dave said dont like it dont watch it

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:49 pm Bah! As a wannabe animator myself, I can't really understand what the appeal (other than trying to draw in the fanboys) would be in throwing things like underage jailbait around (shut your mouth, ShoKenju...) . A series can be successful with 'pretty art'. It doesnt have to contain an overload of sexual humor, or innuendos to make it seem more appealing. But anyways, in the culture they don't really frown upon open sexuality or sexual preferences like most north americans do. Especially in regards to "cartoons". So I suppose sexual humor has become part of what anime is by now, so they toss that into the mix where and when they see it fit. It keeps the fans interested/happy. And the younger the characters are, the funnier it seems to be, apparently. I don't disagree with that kind of content in moderation, simply because it wouldnt change anything. (and I'd have nothing left to watch...) But I do disagree for the reason it's put into a series. ie: giving the fans what they want. But you get desensitized to it after a while, so it's "normal" now. Anything underage, I find disturbing. It's just indecent, in my opinion. No one seems to think of the consequences, nor do they have any common decency or delicacy towards the matter. It's sad that things have to get to that point. But then again, I could be a little more biased. And seeing as it's mostly the younger female characters that get sexualized like this, I'm probably more, for lack of better terms, 'sensitive' towards the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Aug 31, 2004 11:03 pm yeah, what she said

[]:             Quote Post by Super Mega Ultra Deluxe » Tue Aug 31, 2004 11:03 pm zOMG TORRENT PLZ!!1 But yeah, this has bothered me a lot as well (though I don't have a issue with the ShinjiAsuka scene in question). The problem is that this is just very popular and more people like it versus those who are disturbed and are uncomfortable with it. I like collecting toys nd such merhandise from shows and movies, but the thing with Eva is that while there is A LOT of toys most of them are very provactiave and too sexy for me. The problem is that the 'otaku' at GAINAX don't really mind it themselves, and as always, it sells. And as Dave has mentioned, sometimes times in anime characters who aren't old enough to have gone through puberty are sexualized or show sexual interest. And lets not go in interest between relatives which is also a common theme. And sometimes yes art the portays people with unnatural or "older" bodies can be beautiful, but most of the time it's just a perversion of the character. And yes, in my search for pictures I've found many disturbing things.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Tue Aug 31, 2004 11:48 pm ShoKenju wrote: my stomach turned after trying to find Asuka pics for someone go google, "Asuka" images. and look at the first one ><!

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:10 am ShoKenju wrote: go google, "Asuka" images. and look at the first one ><!

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:18 am Anime in itself sexualizes all its minor characters usually for the sake of humor, which I don't mind (Love Hina, Ramna). I do believe that anime is catering to their target audience of 13-17 year olds with 'fan service'. If you are selling to this audience, then sex does sell (for example, look at MTV). I like 'fan service', as long as it's funny or romantic. I'll admit that I watched Evangelion for the first time because I saw Rei, Asuka and Misato in Newtype USA. However, I don't like the hentai or sex-themed merchandise. I may be a guy, but the hentai just cheapens my love of animation in general. Especially in my favorite series. I have avoided Eva hentai movies like the plague. Gainax should be faulted for creating all that stuff (Those sheets with ANY anime character on them are WRONG for sooooo many reasons). They just did it to make more money. What sickens me is that old men with no life (or girlfriend) are buying that stuff. However, I didn't think the sexualization got out of hand in the show or in the movies. The bed scene, for example, in EOE really wasn't there to turn you on. It was there to disgust and shock you about how low Shinji had become. There is also a reason for the scene where Shinji sees Rei naked, which is to show that Rei doesn't have any modesty, or normal human feelings (in a humorous way). There are many pieces of great literature that sexualize minor characters. (Romeo & Juliet, Catcher in the Rye). After all, most of us have sexual feelings by at least 14. It does make it more realistic and emotional. But again, the merchandise takes it too far.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Wed Sep 01, 2004 10:29 am :shock: Well...I, uh...um, I ....uh... humminahumminahummina Ahem, personnaly I think these kind of pics are disrespectful to Evangelion as a series, a peice of art, and to all of its creators. :? But then again, some of its creators actually made some of that stuff so... As for the resin* figures, I dislike them the most. What I'd like to know is what's up with some of those pics from the game! What exactly did GOS2's real story involve?!

[]: Re: Sexualization of Minor characters in Evangelion             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 11:01 am Dave wrote: Let me rephrase that for you to make it more accuracte.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 01, 2004 11:27 am Asuka Langley wrote: And seeing as it's mostly the younger female characters that get sexualized like this,

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:16 pm Dave wrote: Asuka Langley wrote: And seeing as it's mostly the younger female characters that get sexualized like this,

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:59 pm Dave wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: But, let me say I meant to say that a character WILL be a sexual object if they are of the age of puberty.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Wed Sep 01, 2004 3:32 pm It's sad...really sad. Though, I'm not sure if it's because there girls are underaged, or that they are animated. I'm equally likely to make fun of a yaoi-obessed squeeling fangril as I am a drooling fanboy with crusty pants. People take anime on to levels it does not ever need to go to. I know that earlier I ranted about how Gainax made it on to my hate list for exploiting Anno's genius for money and playing to dirty fanboys. At least there weren't any tenticles in NGE or then I might have to really rant... (What the hell is it with Japanese and tenticles and melons?! Is it all part of some crazy symbolism that I shouldn't want to be asking about in the first place?)

[]:             Quote Post by Treeves056 » Wed Sep 01, 2004 4:22 pm Karma Burn wrote: At least there weren't any tenticles in NGE or then I might have to really rant...

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Wed Sep 01, 2004 4:26 pm Don't...DO NOT get me started.... Actually I was referring that there were no tenticles in the actual series...fan art is something different....

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Sep 01, 2004 4:56 pm As far as sexualization of characters in the Evangelion series itself, I think it has every right to be there. Shinji is, after all, a 14 year old boy, and with Evangelion's level of characterization, not bringing up sex wouldn't make sense. As for all the spin-off games and such, it's just there to please the fans, obviously. Karma Burn wrote: (What the hell is it with Japanese and tenticles and melons?! Is it all part of some crazy symbolism that I shouldn't want to be asking about in the first place?)
